title: sabulina striata
intro: Sabulina striata, also known as sea heath, is a low-growing, prostrate shrub native to coastal areas of Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and heal wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The health benefits of Sabulina striata include its potential to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable herb for treating a variety of health conditions. In herbal preparations, Sabulina striata is often used in teas, infusions, and salves, and can be combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and health benefits.
